citation: Schwartz, S. H. (2003). A proposal for measuring value orientations across nations. European Social Survey Core Questionnaire Development.
use_note: Academic research use. PVQ-10 items are published in the ESS core-questionnaire documentation and widely reproduced.
instruction: Here are short descriptions of different people. For each one, tell me how much this person is like you, on a 1 to 6 scale.

items.text: Thinking up new ideas and being creative is important to this person. They like to do things in their own original way.
It is important to this person to be rich. They want to have a lot of money and expensive things.
Living in secure surroundings is important to this person. They avoid anything that might endanger their safety.
It is important to this person to have a good time. They like to "spoil" themselves.
It is important to this person to show their abilities. They want people to admire what they do.
Adventure and taking risks are important to this person. They want to have an exciting life.
It is important to this person to help the people around them. They want to care for their well-being.
It is important to this person always to behave properly. They want to avoid doing anything people would say is wrong.
Tradition is important to this person. They try to follow the customs handed down by their religion or their family.
Looking after the environment is important to this person. They strongly believe people should care for nature.
